preamble: The phonetic transformation from the voiceless postalveolar affricate /tʃ/ to the voiced alveolo-palatal affricate /dʑ/ is a type of lenition, primarily involving the voicing of the affricate. This change can also be accompanied by a slight fronting of the articulation, moving from a postalveolar to an alveolo-palatal place.

examples.note: In some Spanish dialect areas, the voiceless postalveolar affricate /tʃ/ is articulated as a partially or fully voiced affricate, which can be represented as [tʒ] or [tʝ], aligning with a voiced alveolo-palatal affricate [dʑ].

sources: Coloma, G. (2012). The Importance of Ten Phonetic Characteristics to Define Dialect Areas in Spanish. CEMA University, Buenos Aires.
